description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a symmetrical facade and a traditional suburban design. The front features two prominent gabled entryway extensions, each with its own pitched roof, and large rectangular windows that provide ample natural light. The exterior walls are clad in reddish-brown brick, while the roof is finished in dark gray, likely asphalt shingles. The rear of the house opens onto a spacious, fenced backyard with a well-maintained lawn, divided into two sections. The side views reveal additional gabled projections and the continuity of the brickwork, as well as the fenced boundary that encloses the property. The house is surrounded by a low, light-colored fence, and the overall layout suggests a duplex or semi-detached configuration.
description: A two-story brick house with gabled entryways, large windows, and a fenced backyard, featuring a traditional suburban design.
description_view_0: Front view showing two gabled entryways, large windows, and the main facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a single gabled projection and the fenced backyard area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the large, divided backyard and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another gabled projection and a clear view of the backyard fence.
attribute_entryway_design: Twin gabled entryways with overhangs, steps, and large front doors, no porch or columns.
